---
title: "Gyrodinium"
subtitle: "Unarmored dinoflagellate with a strongly displaced, S-shaped (sigmoid) girdle that gives the cell a twisted look."
categories: [Dinoflagellates]
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build_pages.py — do not edit by hand. Edit meta.yml and re-run the script. -->

**Group:** Dinoflagellates  ·  **Optics:** high-mag, low-mag

## Defining characteristics

An athecate ("naked") dinoflagellate whose defining trait is a cingulum (girdle) that is strongly displaced — offset by roughly a fifth of the body length or more — and often wound into an S-shape, so the two halves of the cell (epicone and hypocone) look twisted relative to each other. Cells are usually ovoid to spindle-shaped and lack rigid thecal plates. Key features: naked outline, pronounced sigmoid/displaced girdle, longitudinal surface striae in some species.

## Distinguishing from similar classes

| Similar class | How to tell them apart |
|---|---|
| **akashiwo** | Akashiwo is larger and dorsoventrally flattened with only a slightly offset girdle; Gyrodinium is defined by its strongly displaced, twisted (sigmoid) cingulum. |

## Ideal images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## Challenging images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## References

- [Gyrodinium — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gyrodinium)
- [UCSC Ocean Data Center — Phytoplankton Gallery](http://oceandatacenter.ucsc.edu/PhytoGallery/)
